bosses.name: Margit, the Fell Omen
bosses.lore: A powerful demigod guarding the path to Stormveil Castle. He wields a magical hammer and swift blade techniques.

bosses.name: Godrick the Grafted
bosses.lore: A demigod who grafts the limbs of fallen warriors onto his own body, seeking power he was never born with.

bosses.name: Red Wolf of Radagon
bosses.lore: A magical construct serving as guardian of the academy, wielding sorcery and devastating physical attacks.

bosses.name: Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon
bosses.lore: Former head of the academy and wielder of the Full Moon sorcery, now a broken figure clutching her amber egg.

bosses.name: Starscourge Radahn
bosses.lore: One of the greatest demigods, a warrior who held the stars in place through gravitational sorcery.

bosses.name: Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy
bosses.lore: A demigod who fed himself to a serpent god, merging with it to gain immortality at the cost of his humanity.

bosses.name: Morgott, the Omen King
bosses.lore: The true identity of Margit — an Omen cursed with thorns who locked himself away to protect the Erdtree.

bosses.name: Fire Giant
bosses.lore: The last of a race of giants, kept alive by the Greater Will to guard the Forge of the Giants.

bosses.name: Godskin Duo
bosses.lore: Two members of the Godskin Apostles working in tandem — Noble and Apostle — servants of the Black Blade Kindred.

bosses.name: Maliketh, the Black Blade
bosses.lore: Destined death incarnate, the Black Blade Kindred and shadow of Queen Marika.

bosses.name: Hoarah Loux, Warrior
bosses.lore: The true form of Godfrey, First Elden Lord — a savage chieftain before he was graced by the Erdtree.

bosses.name: Elden Beast
bosses.lore: The vessel of the Elden Ring itself, an outer god that descended from the Erdtree to face the Tarnished.

bosses.name: Malenia, Blade of Miquella
bosses.lore: Universally regarded as the hardest boss in Elden Ring. She has never known defeat, and every hit she lands heals her.

bosses.name: Mohg, Lord of Blood
bosses.lore: An Omen demigod who consorts with the Formless Mother, dreaming of a dynasty built on rivers of blood.

bosses.name: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
bosses.lore: A creature that fell from the stars and obliterated an entire civilisation. It can clone itself and explode stars.

bosses.name: Iudex Gundyr
bosses.lore: A Champion who failed his duty and was impaled with a coiled sword, waiting for the Ashen One to rekindle the flame.

bosses.name: Vordt of the Boreal Valley
bosses.lore: A former knight of the Pontiff turned beast, corrupted by the Sulyvahn's eyes and now serving as a mindless guardian.

bosses.name: Crystal Sage
bosses.lore: A master sorcerer of the Grand Archives who can teleport and split into multiple illusions to confuse attackers.

bosses.name: Deacons of the Deep
bosses.lore: A congregation of clerics who collectively wield a great soul, protecting Aldrich's coffin in the cathedral.

bosses.name: Abyss Watchers
bosses.lore: An order of warriors who hunted the abyss, but one was infected — now they fight each other endlessly.

bosses.name: High Lord Wolnir
bosses.lore: Once a conquering king who feared death above all, he sought the abyss seeking immortality and was consumed by it.

bosses.name: Old Demon King
bosses.lore: The last of the chaos demons, a decrepit king smouldering with dying embers, a relic of the Age of Fire.

bosses.name: Pontiff Sulyvahn
bosses.lore: A sorcerer-king who enslaved Aldrich and Yhorm, and whose magical eyes grant visions of the Profaned Capital.

bosses.name: Yhorm the Giant
bosses.lore: A giant king who took the Profaned Flame to end the suffering of his people. His shield is the tomb of their tragedies.

bosses.name: Aldrich, Devourer of Gods
bosses.lore: A cleric who dreamed of feasting on men and gods alike, eventually consuming the God of the Sun, Gwyndolin.

bosses.name: Dancer of the Boreal Valley
bosses.lore: A former human knight cursed by Sulyvahn and transformed into a graceful but deadly dancer of ash and frost.

bosses.name: Dragonslayer Armour
bosses.lore: The animated armour of a legendary dragonslayer, now empty of its wearer but still driven to protect the path ahead.

bosses.name: Oceiros, the Consumed King
bosses.lore: The king of Lothric who became obsessed with dragons, eventually transforming into a dragon himself.

bosses.name: Champion Gundyr
bosses.lore: Iudex Gundyr in a dark timeline where the fire was never linked — a relentless fighter driven by pure instinct.

bosses.name: Twin Princes
bosses.lore: Lorian and Lothric — the elder brothers the younger, who refuses to link the flame. Two souls fighting as one body.

bosses.name: Soul of Cinder
bosses.lore: The amalgamation of every Lord of Cinder who ever linked the First Flame — the final guardian of the Age of Fire.

bosses.name: Sister Friede
bosses.lore: A former Sable Church member who abandoned everything to protect a painted world, fighting alongside Father Ariandel.

bosses.name: Slave Knight Gael
bosses.lore: A slave knight sent to find the Dark Soul of Humanity for his Lady. He consumed every last dark soul he could find.

bosses.name: Cleric Beast
bosses.lore: A clergyman transformed into a towering beast by the scourge of beasts — the first true horror the Hunter encounters.

bosses.name: Father Gascoigne
bosses.lore: A hunter who succumbed to the scourge mid-hunt. His music box, a gift from his daughter, is the only thing that slows him.

bosses.name: Blood-Starved Beast
bosses.lore: A beast maddened by the old blood, naked and frenzied, haunting the ruins of the forbidden district of Yharnam.

bosses.name: Vicar Amelia
bosses.lore: The head Vicar of the Healing Church, transformed mid-prayer into a massive golden beast clutching a holy pendant.

bosses.name: Shadow of Yharnam
bosses.lore: Three shadow figures who act as guardians of Byrgenwerth, wielding katanas, candles, and serpent sorcery in concert.

bosses.name: Darkbeast Paarl
bosses.lore: An ancient beast crackling with lightning, so old that its flesh has rotted away leaving only a skeleton of electricity.

bosses.name: The Witch of Hemwick
bosses.lore: Twin witches who summon Mad Ones to swarm and distract the Hunter while they remain invisible.

bosses.name: Rom, the Vacuous Spider
bosses.lore: A former student of Byrgenwerth, transformed into a spider. Killing her lifts the veil hiding the Great Ones' presence.

bosses.name: The One Reborn
bosses.lore: A horrific congregation of corpses fused into one massive body, reborn again and again through ritual incantation.

bosses.name: Micolash, Host of the Nightmare
bosses.lore: A scholar who willingly trapped himself in the Hunter's Nightmare to commune with Kos through arcane rituals.

bosses.name: Mergo's Wet Nurse
bosses.lore: A mysterious Great One who tends to Mergo, a child of the Pthumerian queen. It wields multiple spectral blades.

bosses.name: Gehrman, the First Hunter
bosses.lore: The founder of the workshop and master of all hunters, who serves the Moon Presence and guards the Hunter's Dream.

bosses.name: Moon Presence
bosses.lore: A Great One who formed a contract with Gehrman, feeding on human insight and trapping hunters in the dream.

bosses.name: Ludwig, the Holy Blade
bosses.lore: The first hunter of the Healing Church, transformed into a monstrous steed, who reclaims his humanity mid-fight.

bosses.name: Lady Maria of the Astral Clocktower
bosses.lore: A descendant of Queen Yharnam and the finest student of Gehrman, who sealed herself away out of guilt.

bosses.name: Gyoubu Masataka Oniwa
bosses.lore: A fearsome general on horseback who charges with his enormous spear, roaring war cries across the battlefield.

bosses.name: Lady Butterfly
bosses.lore: An aged shinobi master who trained Wolf as a child, now testing him with illusions and phantom butterflies.

bosses.name: Genichiro Ashina
bosses.lore: The grandson of the sword saint, desperately fighting to save Ashina from collapse — by any means necessary.

bosses.name: Guardian Ape
bosses.lore: A massive ape protecting a Lotus of the Palace. Even beheading it is not enough — it rises again headless, wielding its own skull.

bosses.name: Headless Ape
bosses.lore: The Guardian Ape reunited with its mate, fighting as a pair in one of the game's most chaotic duo encounters.

bosses.name: Folding Screen Monkeys
bosses.lore: Four divine monkeys that must be caught through stealth in an illusory realm — hear no evil, see no evil, speak no evil, and death.

bosses.name: Great Shinobi Owl
bosses.lore: Wolf's adoptive father and the master shinobi who trained him — a ruthless pragmatist who puts mission above all.

bosses.name: Emma, the Gentle Blade
bosses.lore: The doctor who healed Wolf and a surrogate daughter to Isshin — she fights only to stop those who would abuse the Dragon's Heritage.

bosses.name: Isshin Ashina
bosses.lore: The legendary founder of Ashina, summoned from death by Emma to stop Wolf from granting immortality to the Divine Heir.

bosses.name: Corrupted Monk
bosses.lore: A monk corrupted by the Fountainhead Waters, an illusion that splits into three phases before the true Monk is revealed.

bosses.name: Divine Dragon
bosses.lore: An ancient dragon from the Divine Realm, the source of the undying power the Dragon's Heritage draws from.

bosses.name: Demon of Hatred
bosses.lore: The lingering hatred of an Ashina general transformed by Shura's fire — a massive demon with sweeping fire attacks.

bosses.name: Sword Saint Isshin
bosses.lore: The true final boss: Genichiro's ultimate gambit summons the young Isshin at the peak of his power, wielding spear, sword, and lightning.
